title: magnolia pulneyensis
intro: Magnolia pulneyensis, also known as the Nilgiri magnolia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Magnoliaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats in southern India. The leaves and bark of Magnolia pulneyensis have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations like decoctions and infusions can be made from the plant, offering relief from respiratory issues and other ailments, and also supporting immune function and digestive health.
